title: SPACE-CLIP: Spatial Perception via Adaptive CLIP Embeddings for Monocular Depth Estimation
summary: Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) has accomplished extraordinary success for semantic understanding but inherently struggles to perceive geometric structure. Existing methods attempt to bridge this gap by querying CLIP with textual prompts, a process that is often indirect and inefficient. This paper introduces a fundamentally different approach using a dual-pathway decoder. We present SPACE-CLIP, an architecture that unlocks and interprets latent geometric knowledge directly from a frozen CLIP vision encoder, completely bypassing the text encoder and its associated textual prompts. A semantic pathway interprets high-level features, dynamically conditioned on global context using feature-wise linear modulation (FiLM). In addition, a structural pathway extracts fine-grained spatial details from early layers. These complementary streams are hierarchically fused, enabling a robust synthesis of semantic context and precise geometry. Extensive experiments on the KITTI benchmark show that SPACE-CLIP dramatically outperforms previous CLIP-based methods. Our ablation studies validate that the synergistic fusion of our dual pathways is critical to this success. SPACE-CLIP offers a new, efficient, and architecturally elegant blueprint for repurposing large-scale vision models. The proposed method is not just a standalone depth estimator, but a readily integrable spatial perception module for the next generation of embodied AI systems, such as vision-language-action (VLA) models. Our model is available at https://github.com/taewan2002/space-clip
tldr: SPACE-CLIP is a novel monocular depth estimation method that extracts geometric knowledge directly from a frozen CLIP vision encoder, bypassing the text encoder and outperforming previous CLIP-based methods by using a dual-pathway decoder to fuse semantic and structural information.
tldr_zh: SPACE-CLIP是一种新颖的单目深度估计方法，它直接从冻结的CLIP视觉编码器中提取几何知识，绕过文本编码器，并通过使用双路径解码器融合语义和结构信息，优于以前基于CLIP的方法。
summary_zh: 对比语言-图像预训练（CLIP）在语义理解方面取得了非凡的成功，但本质上难以感知几何结构。现有的方法试图通过用文本提示查询CLIP来弥补这一差距，但这一过程通常是间接且低效的。本文介绍了一种从根本上不同的方法，使用双通路解码器。我们提出了SPACE-CLIP，该架构可直接从冻结的CLIP视觉编码器中解锁并解释潜在的几何知识，完全绕过文本编码器及其相关的文本提示。语义通路解释高级特征，并使用特征级线性调制（FiLM）动态地以全局上下文为条件。此外，结构通路从早期层中提取精细的空间细节。这些互补的流被分层融合，从而能够稳健地综合语义上下文和精确几何。在KITTI基准上的大量实验表明，SPACE-CLIP显著优于以前基于CLIP的方法。我们的消融研究验证了双通路的协同融合对于这一成功至关重要。SPACE-CLIP为重新利用大规模视觉模型提供了一种新的、高效且架构优雅的蓝图。所提出的方法不仅仅是一个独立的深度估计器，而且是一个易于集成的空间感知模块，适用于下一代具身人工智能系统，例如视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型。我们的模型可在https://github.com/taewan2002/space-clip获取。

title: Masked Depth Modeling for Spatial Perception
summary: Spatial visual perception is a fundamental requirement in physical-world applications like autonomous driving and robotic manipulation, driven by the need to interact with 3D environments. Capturing pixel-aligned metric depth using RGB-D cameras would be the most viable way, yet it usually faces obstacles posed by hardware limitations and challenging imaging conditions, especially in the presence of specular or texture-less surfaces. In this work, we argue that the inaccuracies from depth sensors can be viewed as "masked" signals that inherently reflect underlying geometric ambiguities. Building on this motivation, we present LingBot-Depth, a depth completion model which leverages visual context to refine depth maps through masked depth modeling and incorporates an automated data curation pipeline for scalable training. It is encouraging to see that our model outperforms top-tier RGB-D cameras in terms of both depth precision and pixel coverage. Experimental results on a range of downstream tasks further suggest that LingBot-Depth offers an aligned latent representation across RGB and depth modalities. We release the code, checkpoint, and 3M RGB-depth pairs (including 2M real data and 1M simulated data) to the community of spatial perception.
tldr: The paper introduces LingBot-Depth, a depth completion model that refines depth maps using masked depth modeling and visual context, achieving state-of-the-art performance and improved RGB-depth alignment. They also release a large RGB-depth dataset.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了LingBot-Depth，一个使用掩码深度建模和视觉上下文来优化深度图的深度补全模型，实现了最先进的性能并改善了RGB-depth的对齐。他们还发布了一个大型RGB-depth数据集。
summary_zh: 空间视觉感知是物理世界应用（如自动驾驶和机器人操作）中的一项基本需求，其驱动力在于与3D环境交互的需求。使用RGB-D相机捕获像素对齐的度量深度是最可行的方法，然而它通常面临硬件限制和具有挑战性的成像条件所带来的障碍，尤其是在存在镜面反射或无纹理表面时。在这项工作中，我们认为来自深度传感器的不准确性可以被视为“掩蔽”信号，这些信号本质上反映了底层的几何歧义。基于这种动机，我们提出了LingBot-Depth，一种深度补全模型，它利用视觉上下文，通过掩蔽深度建模来细化深度图，并结合了自动数据管理流程以实现可扩展的训练。令人鼓舞的是，我们的模型在深度精度和像素覆盖率方面均优于顶级RGB-D相机。在一系列下游任务上的实验结果进一步表明，LingBot-Depth提供了RGB和深度模态之间对齐的潜在表征。我们将发布代码、检查点和300万个RGB-深度对（包括200万个真实数据和100万个模拟数据）给空间感知领域社区。

title: PEAfowl: Perception-Enhanced Multi-View Vision-Language-Action for Bimanual Manipulation
summary:
Bimanual manipulation in cluttered scenes requires policies that remain stable under occlusions, viewpoint and scene variations. Existing vision-language-action models often fail to generalize because (i) multi-view features are fused via view-agnostic token concatenation, yielding weak 3D-consistent spatial understanding, and (ii) language is injected as global conditioning, resulting in coarse instruction grounding.
  In this paper, we introduce PEAfowl, a perception-enhanced multi-view VLA policy for bimanual manipulation. For spatial reasoning, PEAfowl predicts per-token depth distributions, performs differentiable 3D lifting, and aggregates local cross-view neighbors to form geometrically grounded, cross-view consistent representations. For instruction grounding, we propose to replace global conditioning with a Perceiver-style text-aware readout over frozen CLIP visual features, enabling iterative evidence accumulation. To overcome noisy and incomplete commodity depth without adding inference overhead, we apply training-only depth distillation from a pretrained depth teacher to supervise the depth-distribution head, providing perception front-end with geometry-aware priors.
  On RoboTwin 2.0 under domain-randomized setting, PEAfowl improves the strongest baseline by 23.0 pp in success rate, and real-robot experiments further demonstrate reliable sim-to-real transfer and consistent improvements from depth distillation.
  Project website: https://peafowlvla.github.io/.
tldr: PEAfowl is a novel vision-language-action model for bimanual manipulation that addresses limitations in spatial understanding and instruction grounding by using per-token depth distributions, differentiable 3D lifting, and a Perceiver-style text-aware readout, leading to improved performance in simulated and real-world robotic tasks.
tldr_zh: PEAfowl 是一种新的用于双手操作的视觉-语言-动作模型，通过使用逐token深度分布、可微3D提升和Perceiver风格的文本感知读取，解决了空间理解和指令对齐方面的局限性，从而提高了模拟和真实世界机器人任务中的性能。
summary_zh:
在杂乱场景中的双臂操作需要策略在遮挡、视角和场景变化下保持稳定。现有的视觉-语言-动作模型通常无法泛化，因为（i）多视角特征通过视角无关的token拼接融合，导致较弱的3D一致性空间理解，以及（ii）语言被注入为全局条件，导致粗糙的指令定位。

在本文中，我们提出了PEAfowl，一种用于双臂操作的感知增强型多视角VLA策略。对于空间推理，PEAfowl预测每个token的深度分布，执行可微的3D提升，并聚合局部跨视角邻域以形成几何对齐的、跨视角一致的表征。对于指令定位，我们建议用基于冻结的CLIP视觉特征的感知器式文本感知读取层来替代全局条件，从而实现迭代证据积累。为了在不增加推理开销的情况下克服嘈杂和不完整的商用深度图，我们应用来自预训练深度教师网络的仅训练时深度蒸馏来监督深度分布头，从而为感知前端提供几何感知的先验。

在领域随机化设置下的RoboTwin 2.0上，PEAfowl将最强基线方法的成功率提高了23.0个百分点，并且真实机器人实验进一步证明了可靠的模拟到真实迁移以及深度蒸馏带来的一致改进。

项目网站：https://peafowlvla.github.io/.

title: Less Is More: Scalable Visual Navigation from Limited Data
summary: Imitation learning provides a powerful framework for goal-conditioned visual navigation in mobile robots, enabling obstacle avoidance while respecting human preferences and social norms. However, its effectiveness depends critically on the quality and diversity of training data. In this work, we show how classical geometric planners can be leveraged to generate synthetic trajectories that complement costly human demonstrations. We train Less is More (LiMo), a transformer-based visual navigation policy that predicts goal-conditioned SE(2) trajectories from a single RGB observation, and find that augmenting limited expert demonstrations with planner-generated supervision yields substantial performance gains. Through ablations and complementary qualitative and quantitative analyses, we characterize how dataset scale and diversity affect planning performance. We demonstrate real-robot deployment and argue that robust visual navigation is enabled not by simply collecting more demonstrations, but by strategically curating diverse, high-quality datasets. Our results suggest that scalable, embodiment-specific geometric supervision is a practical path toward data-efficient visual navigation.
tldr: This paper presents LiMo, a transformer-based visual navigation policy, which leverages synthetic trajectories generated by classical geometric planners to augment limited expert demonstrations, resulting in improved performance in goal-conditioned visual navigation. The results demonstrate that strategically curating diverse, high-quality datasets is more effective than simply collecting more demonstrations.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了LiMo，一种基于Transformer的视觉导航策略，该策略利用经典几何规划器生成的合成轨迹来扩充有限的专家演示，从而提高了基于目标的视觉导航性能。 结果表明，策略性地管理多样化、高质量的数据集比简单地收集更多演示更有效。
summary_zh: 模仿学习为移动机器人中的目标条件视觉导航提供了一个强大的框架，能够在尊重人类偏好和社交规范的同时实现避障。然而，其有效性严重依赖于训练数据的质量和多样性。在这项工作中，我们展示了如何利用经典的几何规划器来生成补充昂贵的人工演示的合成轨迹。我们训练了“少即是多”（LiMo），一种基于 Transformer 的视觉导航策略，该策略从单个 RGB 观测预测目标条件下的 SE(2) 轨迹，并发现利用规划器生成的监督来增强有限的专家演示可以带来显著的性能提升。通过消融实验和补充的定性和定量分析，我们描述了数据集规模和多样性如何影响规划性能。我们展示了在真实机器人上的部署，并论证了稳健的视觉导航不只是通过简单地收集更多演示来实现，而是通过策略性地策划多样化、高质量的数据集来实现。我们的结果表明，可扩展的、针对特定机器人的几何监督是实现数据高效视觉导航的一条可行路径。

title: Agentic AI for Self-Driving Laboratories in Soft Matter: Taxonomy, Benchmarks,and Open Challenges
summary: Self-driving laboratories (SDLs) close the loop between experiment design, automated execution, and data-driven decision making, and they provide a demanding testbed for agentic AI under expensive actions, noisy and delayed feedback, strict feasibility and safety constraints, and non-stationarity. This survey uses soft matter as a representative setting but focuses on the AI questions that arise in real laboratories. We frame SDL autonomy as an agent environment interaction problem with explicit observations, actions, costs, and constraints, and we use this formulation to connect common SDL pipelines to established AI principles. We review the main method families that enable closed loop experimentation, including Bayesian optimization and active learning for sample efficient experiment selection, planning and reinforcement learning for long horizon protocol optimization, and tool using agents that orchestrate heterogeneous instruments and software. We emphasize verifiable and provenance aware policies that support debugging, reproducibility, and safe operation. We then propose a capability driven taxonomy that organizes systems by decision horizon, uncertainty modeling, action parameterization, constraint handling, failure recovery, and human involvement. To enable meaningful comparison, we synthesize benchmark task templates and evaluation metrics that prioritize cost aware performance, robustness to drift, constraint violation behavior, and reproducibility. Finally, we distill lessons from deployed SDLs and outline open challenges in multi-modal representation, calibrated uncertainty, safe exploration, and shared benchmark infrastructure.
tldr: The paper surveys the application of agentic AI in self-driving laboratories (SDLs) for soft matter research, proposing a taxonomy, benchmarks, and outlining open challenges in this domain. It emphasizes verifiable, safe, and reproducible policies, and focuses on bridging the gap between AI methods and real-world experimental settings.
tldr_zh: 本文综述了智能体人工智能在软物质研究的自动驾驶实验室 (SDL) 中的应用，提出了一个分类法、基准，并概述了该领域的开放挑战。它强调可验证、安全和可重复的策略，并侧重于弥合人工智能方法与现实世界实验环境之间的差距。
summary_zh: 自动驾驶实验室 (SDLs) 闭合了实验设计、自动化执行和数据驱动决策之间的循环。它们为智能体人工智能提供了一个严苛的测试平台，涉及昂贵的操作、噪声和延迟反馈、严格的可行性和安全约束，以及非平稳性。本综述以软物质作为代表性场景，但侧重于真实实验室中出现的AI问题。我们将SDL的自主性视为智能体-环境交互问题，其中包含显式的观察、行动、成本和约束，并使用这种公式将常见的SDL流程与已建立的AI原则联系起来。我们回顾了实现闭环实验的主要方法族，包括用于样本高效实验选择的贝叶斯优化和主动学习、用于长时程协议优化的规划和强化学习，以及用于协调异构仪器和软件的工具使用智能体。我们强调可验证的、溯源感知的策略，以支持调试、可重复性和安全操作。然后，我们提出一种能力驱动的分类方法，根据决策时程、不确定性建模、行动参数化、约束处理、故障恢复和人工参与对系统进行组织。为了实现有意义的比较，我们综合了基准任务模板和评估指标，这些指标优先考虑成本敏感型的性能、对漂移的鲁棒性、约束违反行为和可重复性。最后，我们提炼了已部署SDL的经验教训，并概述了多模态表示、校准的不确定性、安全探索和共享基准基础设施方面的开放性挑战。

title: VidLaDA: Bidirectional Diffusion Large Language Models for Efficient Video Understanding
summary: Standard Autoregressive Video LLMs inevitably suffer from causal masking biases that hinder global spatiotemporal modeling, leading to suboptimal understanding efficiency. We propose VidLaDA, a Video LLM based on Diffusion Language Model utilizing bidirectional attention to capture bidirectional dependencies. To further tackle the inference bottleneck of diffusion decoding on massive video tokens, we introduce MARS-Cache. This framework accelerates inference by combining asynchronous visual cache refreshing with frame-wise chunk attention, effectively pruning redundancy while preserving global connectivity via anchor tokens. Extensive experiments show VidLaDA outperforms diffusion baselines and rivals state-of-the-art autoregressive models (e.g., Qwen2.5-VL and LLaVA-Video), with MARS-Cache delivering over 12x speedup without compromising reasoning accuracy. Code and checkpoints are open-sourced at https://github.com/ziHoHe/VidLaDA.
tldr: VidLaDA is a novel Video LLM that utilizes a bidirectional diffusion language model and MARS-Cache for efficient video understanding, outperforming strong autoregressive baselines with significant speedup.
tldr_zh: VidLaDA是一种新型视频LLM，它利用双向扩散语言模型和MARS-Cache来实现高效的视频理解，在显著加速的同时，优于强大的自回归基线模型。
summary_zh: 标准自回归视频LLM不可避免地受到因果掩码偏差的影响，阻碍了全局时空建模，导致次优的理解效率。我们提出了VidLaDA，一种基于扩散语言模型的视频LLM，它利用双向注意力来捕获双向依赖关系。为了进一步解决大规模视频令牌的扩散解码推理瓶颈，我们引入了MARS-Cache。该框架通过结合异步视觉缓存刷新和逐帧块注意力来加速推理，有效地修剪冗余，同时通过锚定令牌保留全局连接。大量实验表明，VidLaDA性能优于扩散基线，并与最先进的自回归模型（例如，Qwen2.5-VL和LLaVA-Video）相媲美，而MARS-Cache在不影响推理准确性的情况下，提供了超过12倍的加速。代码和检查点已在https://github.com/ziHoHe/VidLaDA开源。

title: Automated structural testing of LLM-based agents: methods, framework, and case studies
summary: LLM-based agents are rapidly being adopted across diverse domains. Since they interact with users without supervision, they must be tested extensively. Current testing approaches focus on acceptance-level evaluation from the user's perspective. While intuitive, these tests require manual evaluation, are difficult to automate, do not facilitate root cause analysis, and incur expensive test environments. In this paper, we present methods to enable structural testing of LLM-based agents. Our approach utilizes traces (based on OpenTelemetry) to capture agent trajectories, employs mocking to enforce reproducible LLM behavior, and adds assertions to automate test verification. This enables testing agent components and interactions at a deeper technical level within automated workflows. We demonstrate how structural testing enables the adaptation of software engineering best practices to agents, including the test automation pyramid, regression testing, test-driven development, and multi-language testing. In representative case studies, we demonstrate automated execution and faster root-cause analysis. Collectively, these methods reduce testing costs and improve agent quality through higher coverage, reusability, and earlier defect detection. We provide an open source reference implementation on GitHub.
tldr: This paper introduces an automated structural testing framework for LLM-based agents using tracing, mocking, and assertions to improve testing coverage, reusability, and defect detection, reducing testing costs.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个针对基于LLM的代理的自动化结构测试框架，该框架使用追踪、模拟和断言来提高测试覆盖率、可重用性和缺陷检测，从而降低测试成本。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型（LLM）的智能体正在迅速被应用于各个领域。由于它们在无监督的情况下与用户交互，因此必须进行广泛的测试。目前的测试方法侧重于从用户角度进行的接受度级别评估。虽然直观，但这些测试需要手动评估，难以自动化，不利于根本原因分析，并且需要昂贵的测试环境。在本文中，我们提出了实现基于LLM的智能体的结构化测试的方法。我们的方法利用跟踪（基于OpenTelemetry）来捕获智能体轨迹，采用模拟（mocking）来强制保证LLM行为的可复现性，并添加断言来实现测试验证的自动化。这使得在自动化工作流程中，能够在更深的技术层面测试智能体组件和交互。我们展示了结构化测试如何使软件工程的最佳实践适应于智能体，包括测试自动化金字塔、回归测试、测试驱动开发和多语言测试。在代表性的案例研究中，我们展示了自动执行和更快的根本原因分析。总而言之，这些方法通过更高的覆盖率、可重用性和更早的缺陷检测，降低了测试成本并提高了智能体的质量。我们在GitHub上提供了一个开源参考实现。

title: EntWorld: A Holistic Environment and Benchmark for Verifiable Enterprise GUI Agents
summary: Recent advances in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have enabled agents to operate in open-ended web and operating system environments. However, existing benchmarks predominantly target consumer-oriented scenarios (e.g., e-commerce and travel booking), failing to capture the complexity and rigor of professional enterprise workflows. Enterprise systems pose distinct challenges, including high-density user interfaces, strict business logic constraints, and a strong reliance on precise, state-consistent information retrieval-settings in which current generalist agents often struggle. To address this gap, we introduce EntWorld, a large-scale benchmark consisting of 1,756 tasks across six representative enterprise domains, including customer relationship management (CRM), information technology infrastructure library (ITIL), and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. Unlike previous datasets that depend on fragile execution traces or extensive manual annotation, EntWorld adopts a schema-grounded task generation framework that directly reverse-engineers business logic from underlying database schemas, enabling the synthesis of realistic, long-horizon workflows. Moreover, we propose a SQL-based deterministic verification mechanism in building datasets that replaces ambiguous visual matching with rigorous state-transition validation. Experimental results demonstrate that state-of-the-art models (e.g., GPT-4.1) achieve 47.61% success rate on EntWorld, substantially lower than the human performance, highlighting a pronounced enterprise gap in current agentic capabilities and the necessity of developing domain-specific agents. We release EntWorld as a rigorous testbed to facilitate the development and evaluation of the next generation of enterprise-ready digital agents.
tldr: The paper introduces EntWorld, a new benchmark for evaluating enterprise GUI agents, highlighting the significant performance gap of current models in complex, real-world enterprise scenarios and providing a schema-grounded, verifiable task generation framework.
tldr_zh: 这篇论文介绍了EntWorld，一个新的用于评估企业GUI代理的基准，强调了当前模型在复杂、真实的企场景中存在的显著性能差距，并提供了一个基于模式的、可验证的任务生成框架。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）的最新进展使智能体能够在开放式的网络和操作系统环境中运行。然而，现有的基准测试主要针对面向消费者的场景（例如，电子商务和旅游预订），未能捕捉到专业企业工作流程的复杂性和严谨性。企业系统带来了独特的挑战，包括高密度用户界面、严格的业务逻辑约束以及对精确、状态一致信息检索的强烈依赖——在这些设置下，当前通用型智能体常常表现不佳。为了弥补这一差距，我们推出了EntWorld，一个大规模基准测试，包含跨六个代表性企业领域的1756个任务，包括客户关系管理（CRM）、信息技术基础设施库（ITIL）和企业资源计划（ERP）系统。与依赖脆弱的执行轨迹或大量人工标注的先前数据集不同，EntWorld采用了一种模式驱动的任务生成框架，该框架直接从底层数据库模式逆向工程业务逻辑，从而能够合成逼真的、长时程的工作流程。此外，我们提出了一种基于SQL的确定性验证机制来构建数据集，该机制用严格的状态转换验证代替了模糊的视觉匹配。实验结果表明，最先进的模型（例如，GPT-4.1）在EntWorld上实现了47.61%的成功率，远低于人类的表现，突显了当前智能体能力中一个明显的企业差距，以及开发特定领域智能体的必要性。我们发布EntWorld作为一个严格的测试平台，以促进下一代企业级数字智能体的开发和评估。

title: SQL-Trail: Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning with Interleaved Feedback for Text-to-SQL
summary: While large language models (LLMs) have substantially improved Text-to-SQL generation, a pronounced gap remains between AI systems and human experts on challenging benchmarks such as BIRD-SQL. We argue this gap stems largely from the prevailing single-pass paradigm, which lacks the iterative reasoning, schema exploration, and error-correction behaviors that humans naturally employ. To address this limitation, we introduce SQL-Trail, a multi-turn reinforcement learning (RL) agentic framework for Text-to-SQL. Rather than producing a query in one shot, SQL-Trail interacts with the database environment and uses execution feedback to iteratively refine its predictions. Our approach centers on two key ideas: (i) an adaptive turn-budget allocation mechanism that scales the agent's interaction depth to match question difficulty, and (ii) a composite reward panel that jointly incentivizes SQL correctness and efficient exploration. Across benchmarks, SQL-Trail sets a new state of the art and delivers strong data efficiency--up to 18x higher than prior single-pass RL state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our 7B and 14B models outperform substantially larger proprietary systems by 5% on average, underscoring the effectiveness of interactive, agentic workflows for robust Text-to-SQL generation.
tldr: SQL-Trail introduces a multi-turn reinforcement learning framework for Text-to-SQL, utilizing interleaved feedback and adaptive turn-budget allocation to achieve state-of-the-art performance and data efficiency compared to single-pass methods, even surpassing larger proprietary models.
tldr_zh: SQL-Trail 提出了一个用于 Text-to-SQL 的多轮强化学习框架，该框架利用交错反馈和自适应回合预算分配，实现了最先进的性能和数据效率，超越了单通道方法，甚至超过了更大的专有模型。
summary_zh: 虽然大型语言模型(LLMs)已大幅改进了文本到SQL的生成，但在诸如BIRD-SQL等具有挑战性的基准测试中，AI系统与人类专家之间仍然存在显著差距。我们认为，这种差距主要源于目前流行的单次生成范式，该范式缺乏人类自然采用的迭代推理、模式探索和错误纠正行为。为了解决这个局限性，我们引入了SQL-Trail，一个用于文本到SQL的多轮强化学习(RL)智能体框架。SQL-Trail不采用一次性生成查询的方式，而是与数据库环境交互并利用执行反馈来迭代地优化其预测。我们的方法主要围绕两个关键思想：(i) 一种自适应的轮次预算分配机制，该机制根据问题的难度来调整智能体的交互深度，以及 (ii) 一个复合奖励面板，该面板共同激励SQL的正确性和高效探索。在多个基准测试中，SQL-Trail 创造了新的技术水平，并提供了强大的数据效率——高达先前单次RL最优方法的18倍。值得注意的是，我们的7B和14B模型平均比更大的专有系统表现高出5%，突出了交互式的智能体工作流在健壮的文本到SQL生成中的有效性。

title: Agentic reinforcement learning empowers next-generation chemical language models for molecular design and synthesis
summary: Language models are revolutionizing the biochemistry domain, assisting scientists in drug design and chemical synthesis with high efficiency. Yet current approaches struggle between small language models prone to hallucination and limited knowledge retention, and large cloud-based language models plagued by privacy risks and high inference costs. To bridge this gap, we introduce ChemCRAFT, a novel framework leveraging agentic reinforcement learning to decouple chemical reasoning from knowledge storage. Instead of forcing the model to memorize vast chemical data, our approach empowers the language model to interact with a sandbox for precise information retrieval. This externalization of knowledge allows a locally deployable small model to achieve superior performance with minimal inference costs. To enable small language models for agent-calling ability, we build an agentic trajectory construction pipeline and a comprehensive chemical-agent sandbox. Based on sandbox interactions, we constructed ChemToolDataset, the first large-scale chemical tool trajectory dataset. Simultaneously, we propose SMILES-GRPO to build a dense chemical reward function, promoting the model's ability to call chemical agents. Evaluations across diverse aspects of drug design show that ChemCRAFT outperforms current cloud-based LLMs in molecular structure analysis, molecular optimization, and synthesis pathway prediction, demonstrating that scientific reasoning is not solely an emergent ability of model scale, but a learnable policy of tool orchestration. This work establishes a cost-effective and privacy-preserving paradigm for AI-aided chemistry, opening new avenues for accelerating molecular discovery with locally deployable agents.
tldr: The paper introduces ChemCRAFT, a framework using agentic reinforcement learning to enable small, locally deployable language models to outperform larger, cloud-based models in molecular design and synthesis by decoupling chemical reasoning from knowledge storage.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ChemCRAFT，一个利用Agentic强化学习的框架，通过将化学推理与知识存储分离，使小型本地部署的语言模型在分子设计和合成方面优于大型云模型。
summary_zh: 语言模型正以高效率助力药物设计和化学合成，从而革新生物化学领域。然而，当前的方法面临着小型语言模型易产生幻觉且知识保留有限，以及大型云端语言模型存在隐私风险和高昂推理成本的困境。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种新颖的框架 ChemCRAFT，它利用基于智能体的强化学习将化学推理与知识存储解耦。我们的方法不是强迫模型记忆大量的化学数据，而是使语言模型能够与沙箱进行交互，以进行精确的信息检索。这种知识外化使本地可部署的小型模型能够以最小的推理成本实现卓越的性能。为了使小型语言模型具备智能体调用能力，我们构建了一个智能体轨迹构建流程和一个全面的化学智能体沙箱。基于沙箱交互，我们构建了 ChemToolDataset，这是首个大规模化学工具轨迹数据集。同时，我们提出了 SMILES-GRPO 来构建密集的化学奖励函数，从而提高模型调用化学智能体的能力。在药物设计各个方面的评估表明，ChemCRAFT 在分子结构分析、分子优化和合成路径预测方面优于当前的云端 LLM，表明科学推理不仅仅是模型规模涌现出的能力，而是可学习的工具编排策略。这项工作为人工智能辅助化学建立了一个经济高效且保护隐私的范式，为利用本地可部署的智能体加速分子发现开辟了新的途径。

title: Benchmarking Direct Preference Optimization for Medical Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) hold significant promise for medical applications, yet their deployment is often constrained by insufficient alignment and reliability. While Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has emerged as a potent framework for refining model responses, its efficacy in high-stakes medical contexts remains underexplored, lacking the rigorous empirical groundwork necessary to guide future methodological advances. To bridge this gap, we present the first comprehensive examination of diverse DPO variants within the medical domain, evaluating nine distinct formulations across two medical LVLMs: LLaVA-Med and HuatuoGPT-Vision. Our results reveal several critical limitations: current DPO approaches often yield inconsistent gains over supervised fine-tuning, with their efficacy varying significantly across different tasks and backbones. Furthermore, they frequently fail to resolve fundamental visual misinterpretation errors. Building on these insights, we present a targeted preference construction strategy as a proof-of-concept that explicitly addresses visual misinterpretation errors frequently observed in existing DPO models. This design yields a 3.6% improvement over the strongest existing DPO baseline on visual question-answering tasks. To support future research, we release our complete framework, including all training data, model checkpoints, and our codebase at https://github.com/dmis-lab/med-vlm-dpo.
tldr: This paper benchmarks various Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) formulations for medical Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), identifies limitations, and proposes a targeted preference construction strategy to address visual misinterpretations, showing a 3.6% improvement on visual question answering.
tldr_zh: 该论文对医学大型视觉语言模型(LVLMs)的多种直接偏好优化(DPO)公式进行了基准测试，指出了局限性，并提出了一种有针对性的偏好构建策略，以解决视觉误解问题，在视觉问答方面取得了3.6%的改进。
summary_zh: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在医学应用方面具有巨大潜力，但其部署常常受到一致性和可靠性不足的限制。虽然直接偏好优化（DPO）已经成为一种用于优化模型响应的有效框架，但其在高风险医疗环境下的有效性仍未得到充分探索，缺乏指导未来方法学进展所需的严格实证基础。为了弥合这一差距，我们首次对医学领域中各种DPO变体进行了全面考察，评估了两种医学LVLM（LLaVA-Med和HuatuoGPT-Vision）的九种不同配方。我们的结果揭示了几个关键局限性：当前的DPO方法通常无法获得优于监督微调的一致收益，并且其有效性在不同任务和骨干网络之间差异显著。此外，它们经常无法解决根本性的视觉误解错误。基于这些见解，我们提出了一种有针对性的偏好构建策略，作为一种概念验证，明确解决了现有DPO模型中经常观察到的视觉误解错误。这种设计在视觉问答任务上比现有的最强DPO基线提高了3.6%。为了支持未来的研究，我们发布了完整的框架，包括所有训练数据、模型检查点以及代码库，网址为：https://github.com/dmis-lab/med-vlm-dpo。

title: ViTCoP: Accelerating Large Vision-Language Models via Visual and Textual Semantic Collaborative Pruning
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) incur high computational costs due to significant redundancy in their visual tokens. To effectively reduce this cost, researchers have proposed various visual token pruning methods. However, existing methods are generally limited, either losing critical visual information prematurely due to pruning in the vision encoder, or leading to information redundancy among the selected tokens due to pruning in the Large Language Models (LLMs). To address these challenges, we propose a Visual and Textual Semantic Collaborative Pruning framework (ViTCoP) that combines redundancy filtering in the vision encoder with step-wise co-pruning within the LLM based on its hierarchical characteristics, to efficiently preserve critical and informationally diverse visual tokens. Meanwhile, to ensure compatibility with acceleration techniques like FlashAttention, we introduce the L2 norm of K-vectors as the token saliency metric in the LLM. Extensive experiments on various Large Vision-Language Models demonstrate that ViTCoP not only achieves state-of-the-art performance surpassing existing methods on both image and video understanding tasks, but also significantly reduces model inference latency and GPU memory consumption. Notably, its performance advantage over other methods becomes even more pronounced under extreme pruning rates.
tldr: The paper introduces ViTCoP, a visual and textual semantic collaborative pruning framework for LVLMs that reduces computational costs by intelligently pruning visual tokens in both the vision encoder and LLM, achieving state-of-the-art performance with reduced latency and memory consumption.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为ViTCoP的视觉和文本语义协作剪枝框架，用于大型视觉语言模型，通过在视觉编码器和LLM中智能剪枝视觉tokens来降低计算成本，并在降低延迟和内存消耗的同时实现最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）因其视觉标记中的大量冗余而产生高昂的计算成本。为了有效降低此成本，研究人员提出了各种视觉标记剪枝方法。然而，现有方法通常存在局限性，要么由于在视觉编码器中进行剪枝而过早丢失关键视觉信息，要么由于在大语言模型（LLMs）中进行剪枝而导致所选标记间的信息冗余。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一个视觉和文本语义协同剪枝框架（ViTCoP），该框架结合了视觉编码器中的冗余过滤以及LLM内部基于其层次结构的逐步协同剪枝，以高效地保留关键且信息多样的视觉标记。同时，为了确保与诸如FlashAttention之类的加速技术兼容，我们在LLM中引入了K向量的L2范数作为标记显著性度量。在各种大型视觉语言模型上的大量实验表明，ViTCoP不仅在图像和视频理解任务上取得了超越现有技术的最佳性能，而且显著降低了模型推理延迟和GPU内存消耗。值得注意的是，在极端剪枝率下，其相对于其他方法的性能优势更加明显。

title: Sponge Tool Attack: Stealthy Denial-of-Efficiency against Tool-Augmented Agentic Reasoning
summary: Enabling large language models (LLMs) to solve complex reasoning tasks is a key step toward artificial general intelligence. Recent work augments LLMs with external tools to enable agentic reasoning, achieving high utility and efficiency in a plug-and-play manner. However, the inherent vulnerabilities of such methods to malicious manipulation of the tool-calling process remain largely unexplored. In this work, we identify a tool-specific attack surface and propose Sponge Tool Attack (STA), which disrupts agentic reasoning solely by rewriting the input prompt under a strict query-only access assumption. Without any modification on the underlying model or the external tools, STA converts originally concise and efficient reasoning trajectories into unnecessarily verbose and convoluted ones before arriving at the final answer. This results in substantial computational overhead while remaining stealthy by preserving the original task semantics and user intent. To achieve this, we design STA as an iterative, multi-agent collaborative framework with explicit rewritten policy control, and generates benign-looking prompt rewrites from the original one with high semantic fidelity. Extensive experiments across 6 models (including both open-source models and closed-source APIs), 12 tools, 4 agentic frameworks, and 13 datasets spanning 5 domains validate the effectiveness of STA.
tldr: The paper introduces the Sponge Tool Attack (STA), a novel method to disrupt LLM agentic reasoning by subtly rewriting prompts, causing increased computational overhead without altering the task semantics or user intent. It demonstrates STA's effectiveness across various models, tools, and datasets.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了海绵工具攻击 (STA)，一种通过微妙地重写提示来扰乱 LLM 智能体推理的新方法，导致计算开销增加，但不会改变任务语义或用户意图。它证明了 STA 在各种模型、工具和数据集中的有效性
summary_zh: 使大型语言模型（LLM）能够解决复杂的推理任务是迈向通用人工智能的关键一步。最近的研究工作利用外部工具增强LLM，以实现代理式推理，以即插即用的方式实现高实用性和高效率。 然而，这种方法对工具调用过程的恶意操控的固有脆弱性，在很大程度上仍未被探索。在这项工作中，我们识别出一个特定于工具的攻击面，并提出了海绵工具攻击（STA），该攻击仅通过在严格的仅查询访问假设下重写输入提示来破坏代理式推理。在不对底层模型或外部工具进行任何修改的情况下，STA将原本简洁高效的推理轨迹转换为不必要的冗长且复杂的轨迹，然后得出最终答案。这导致了巨大的计算开销，同时通过保留原始的任务语义和用户意图来保持隐蔽性。为了实现这一点，我们将STA设计为一个迭代的、多代理协作框架，具有显式的重写策略控制，并从原始提示生成具有高语义保真度的看似良性的提示重写。跨越6个模型（包括开源模型和闭源API）、12个工具、4个代理框架，以及涵盖5个领域的13个数据集的大量实验验证了STA的有效性。

title: Quantum-Inspired Episode Selection for Monte Carlo Reinforcement Learning via QUBO Optimization
summary: Monte Carlo (MC) reinforcement learning suffers from high sample complexity, especially in environments with sparse rewards, large state spaces, and correlated trajectories. We address these limitations by reformulating episode selection as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem and solving it with quantum-inspired samplers. Our method, MC+QUBO, integrates a combinatorial filtering step into standard MC policy evaluation: from each batch of trajectories, we select a subset that maximizes cumulative reward while promoting state-space coverage. This selection is encoded as a QUBO, where linear terms favor high-reward episodes and quadratic terms penalize redundancy. We explore both Simulated Quantum Annealing (SQA) and Simulated Bifurcation (SB) as black-box solvers within this framework. Experiments in a finite-horizon GridWorld demonstrate that MC+QUBO outperforms vanilla MC in convergence speed and final policy quality, highlighting the potential of quantum-inspired optimization as a decision-making subroutine in reinforcement learning.
tldr: The paper introduces MC+QUBO, a Monte Carlo Reinforcement Learning method enhanced by reformulating episode selection as a QUBO problem and solving it with quantum-inspired samplers, demonstrating improved convergence and policy quality in a GridWorld environment.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 MC+QUBO，一种蒙特卡洛强化学习方法，通过将 episode 选择重新表述为 QUBO 问题并使用量子启发式采样器来解决它，从而增强了该方法，并在 GridWorld 环境中展示了改进的收敛性和策略质量。
summary_zh: 蒙特卡洛(MC)强化学习存在样本复杂度高的问题，尤其是在具有稀疏奖励、大型状态空间和相关轨迹的环境中。我们通过将episode选择重新定义为二次无约束二元优化(QUBO)问题，并使用量子启发式采样器求解来解决这些限制。我们的方法，MC+QUBO，将一个组合滤波步骤集成到标准MC策略评估中：从每批轨迹中，我们选择一个子集，使其在最大化累积奖励的同时，促进状态空间覆盖。这种选择被编码为一个QUBO，其中线性项倾向于高回报的episode，而二次项惩罚冗余。我们探索了模拟量子退火(SQA)和模拟分岔(SB)作为该框架内的黑盒求解器。在有限视界的GridWorld环境中的实验表明，MC+QUBO在收敛速度和最终策略质量方面优于原生MC，突出了量子启发优化作为强化学习中决策子程序的潜力。

title: Correct-by-Construction Vision-based Pose Estimation using Geometric Generative Models
summary: We consider the problem of vision-based pose estimation for autonomous systems. While deep neural networks have been successfully used for vision-based tasks, they inherently lack provable guarantees on the correctness of their output, which is crucial for safety-critical applications. We present a framework for designing certifiable neural networks (NNs) for perception-based pose estimation that integrates physics-driven modeling with learning-based estimation. The proposed framework begins by leveraging the known geometry of planar objects commonly found in the environment, such as traffic signs and runway markings, referred to as target objects. At its core, it introduces a geometric generative model (GGM), a neural-network-like model whose parameters are derived from the image formation process of a target object observed by a camera. Once designed, the GGM can be used to train NN-based pose estimators with certified guarantees in terms of their estimation errors. We first demonstrate this framework in uncluttered environments, where the target object is the only object present in the camera's field of view. We extend this using ideas from NN reachability analysis to design certified object NN that can detect the presence of the target object in cluttered environments. Subsequently, the framework consolidates the certified object detector with the certified pose estimator to design a multi-stage perception pipeline that generalizes the proposed approach to cluttered environments, while maintaining its certified guarantees. We evaluate the proposed framework using both synthetic and real images of various planar objects commonly encountered by autonomous vehicles. Using images captured by an event-based camera, we show that the trained encoder can effectively estimate the pose of a traffic sign in accordance with the certified bound provided by the framework.
tldr: This paper presents a framework for designing certifiable neural networks for vision-based pose estimation using geometric generative models, focusing on safety-critical applications where provable guarantees are important. The method is demonstrated on planar objects in both uncluttered and cluttered environments, evaluated using synthetic and real images.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种使用几何生成模型设计可认证神经网络的框架，用于基于视觉的姿态估计，重点关注安全关键应用，其中可证明的保证非常重要。该方法在无杂波和杂波环境中对平面物体进行了演示，并使用合成和真实图像进行了评估。
summary_zh: 我们考虑面向自主系统的基于视觉的位姿估计问题。虽然深度神经网络已成功应用于基于视觉的任务，但其本质上缺乏对其输出正确性的可证明保证，这对于安全攸关的应用至关重要。我们提出了一个框架，用于设计可认证的神经网络（NN），以用于基于感知的位姿估计，该框架将物理驱动的建模与基于学习的估计相结合。该提出的框架首先利用环境中常见的平面对象的已知几何结构，例如交通标志和跑道标记，这些对象被称为目标对象。其核心在于，它引入了一个几何生成模型（GGM），这是一种类似神经网络的模型，其参数源自相机观察目标对象的图像形成过程。一旦设计完成，GGM可用于训练基于NN的位姿估计器，并在估计误差方面具有可认证的保证。我们首先在非杂乱环境中展示了该框架，在该环境中，目标对象是相机视野中唯一存在的对象。我们利用来自NN可达性分析的思想对其进行扩展，以设计可认证的对象NN，其可以检测杂乱环境中目标对象的存在。随后，该框架将可认证的对象检测器与可认证的位姿估计器相结合，以设计一个多阶段感知流水线，从而将所提出的方法推广到杂乱环境中，同时保持其可认证的保证。我们使用自主车辆常见的各种平面对象的合成图像和真实图像来评估所提出的框架。使用事件相机捕获的图像，我们表明训练的编码器可以根据该框架提供的可认证边界有效地估计交通标志的位姿。

title: AsterNav: Autonomous Aerial Robot Navigation In Darkness Using Passive Computation
summary: Autonomous aerial navigation in absolute darkness is crucial for post-disaster search and rescue operations, which often occur from disaster-zone power outages. Yet, due to resource constraints, tiny aerial robots, perfectly suited for these operations, are unable to navigate in the darkness to find survivors safely. In this paper, we present an autonomous aerial robot for navigation in the dark by combining an Infra-Red (IR) monocular camera with a large-aperture coded lens and structured light without external infrastructure like GPS or motion-capture. Our approach obtains depth-dependent defocus cues (each structured light point appears as a pattern that is depth dependent), which acts as a strong prior for our AsterNet deep depth estimation model. The model is trained in simulation by generating data using a simple optical model and transfers directly to the real world without any fine-tuning or retraining. AsterNet runs onboard the robot at 20 Hz on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin$^\text{TM}$ Nano. Furthermore, our network is robust to changes in the structured light pattern and relative placement of the pattern emitter and IR camera, leading to simplified and cost-effective construction. We successfully evaluate and demonstrate our proposed depth navigation approach AsterNav using depth from AsterNet in many real-world experiments using only onboard sensing and computation, including dark matte obstacles and thin ropes (diameter 6.25mm), achieving an overall success rate of 95.5% with unknown object shapes, locations and materials. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work on monocular, structured-light-based quadrotor navigation in absolute darkness.
tldr: The paper introduces AsterNav, an autonomous aerial robot navigation system for complete darkness using a monocular IR camera, coded lens, and structured light, achieving 95.5% success in real-world experiments without retraining.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为AsterNav的自主空中机器人在完全黑暗中导航的系统。该系统使用单目红外相机、编码镜头和结构光，无需重新训练即可在真实环境中实验实现95.5%的成功率。
summary_zh: 绝对黑暗环境下的自主空中导航对于灾后搜救行动至关重要，这些行动通常发生在灾区电力中断的情况下。然而，由于资源限制，非常适合这些行动的微型空中机器人无法在黑暗中安全地导航以寻找幸存者。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于黑暗环境下导航的自主空中机器人，它将红外（IR）单目相机与大孔径编码透镜和结构光相结合，无需GPS या运动捕捉等外部基础设施。我们的方法获取深度相关的散焦线索（每个结构光点都呈现出一种深度相关的图案），这为我们的AsterNet深度深度估计模型提供了一个强大的先验。该模型通过使用简单的光学模型生成数据在仿真环境中进行训练，并直接迁移到真实世界，无需任何微调或重新训练。AsterNet在机器人上运行，在NVIDIA Jetson Orin$^\text{TM}$ Nano上以20 Hz的频率运行。此外，我们的网络对结构光图案和图案发射器与红外相机的相对位置的变化具有鲁棒性，从而简化了构造并降低了成本。我们成功地评估并演示了我们提出的深度导航方法AsterNav，该方法在许多真实世界的实验中使用来自AsterNet的深度数据，仅使用机载传感和计算，包括黑暗漫反射障碍物和细绳（直径6.25毫米），在未知物体形状、位置和材料的情况下，实现了95.5%的总体成功率。据我们所知，这是第一项基于单目结构光的四旋翼飞行器在绝对黑暗中导航的研究。

title: Learning Transferable Skills in Action RPGs via Directed Skill Graphs and Selective Adaptation
summary: Lifelong agents should expand their competence over time without retraining from scratch or overwriting previously learned behaviors. We investigate this in a challenging real-time control setting (Dark Souls III) by representing combat as a directed skill graph and training its components in a hierarchical curriculum. The resulting agent decomposes control into five reusable skills: camera control, target lock-on, movement, dodging, and a heal-attack decision policy, each optimized for a narrow responsibility. This factorization improves sample efficiency by reducing the burden on any single policy and supports selective post-training: when the environment shifts from Phase 1 to Phase 2, only a subset of skills must be adapted, while upstream skills remain transferable. Empirically, we find that targeted fine-tuning of just two skills rapidly recovers performance under a limited interaction budget, suggesting that skill-graph curricula together with selective fine-tuning offer a practical pathway toward evolving, continually learning agents in complex real-time environments.
tldr: The paper presents a hierarchical reinforcement learning approach using directed skill graphs to decompose control in Action RPGs (Dark Souls III). Selective fine-tuning of skills allows for efficient adaptation to changing environments, demonstrating a step towards continual learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种使用有向技能图的分层强化学习方法，以分解动作角色扮演游戏（黑暗之魂 III）中的控制。通过对技能进行选择性微调，可以有效地适应不断变化的环境，展示了持续学习的一步。
summary_zh: 终身学习智能体应能随着时间推移扩展其能力，而无需从头开始重新训练或覆盖先前学习的行为。我们在一个具有挑战性的实时控制环境中（黑暗之魂III）对此进行了研究，通过将战斗表示为一个有向技能图，并在分层课程中训练其组成部分。由此产生的智能体将控制分解为五个可重用的技能：相机控制、目标锁定、移动、闪避和一个治疗-攻击决策策略，每个技能都针对狭窄的职责进行优化。这种分解通过减少对任何单个策略的负担来提高样本效率，并支持选择性后训练：当环境从第一阶段转变到第二阶段时，只需调整部分技能，而上游技能仍然可以转移。经验表明，在有限的交互预算下，仅对两个技能进行有针对性的微调即可迅速恢复性能，这表明技能图课程与选择性微调共同为在复杂的实时环境中发展、持续学习的智能体提供了一条可行的途径。

title: Differential Voting: Loss Functions For Axiomatically Diverse Aggregation of Heterogeneous Preferences
summary: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) implicitly aggregates heterogeneous human preferences into a single utility function, even though the underlying utilities of the participants are in practice diverse. Hence, RLHF can be viewed as a form of voting, where the aggregation mechanism is defined by the loss function. Although Arrow's Impossibility Theorem suggests that different mechanisms satisfy different sets of desirable axioms, most existing methods rely on a single aggregation principle, typically the Bradley-Terry-Luce (BTL) model, which corresponds to Borda count voting. This restricts the axiomatic properties of the learned reward and obscures the normative assumptions embedded in optimization. In this work, we introduce Differential Voting, a unifying framework that constructs instance-wise, differentiable loss functions whose population-level optima provably correspond to distinct classical voting rules. We develop differentiable surrogates for majority-based aggregation (BTL), Copeland, and Kemeny rules, and formally analyze their calibration properties, gradient fields, and limiting behavior as smoothing parameters vanish. For each loss, we establish consistency with the corresponding social choice rule and characterize the axioms it satisfies or violates. Our analysis shows how design choices in loss geometry-such as margin sensitivity and boundary concentration-directly translate into normative aggregation behavior. Differential Voting makes preference aggregation an explicit and controllable design choice in RLHF, enabling principled trade-offs between axiomatic guarantees and optimization stability. Code to reproduce our experiments is open-sourced.
tldr: This paper introduces Differential Voting, a framework for designing differentiable loss functions in RLHF that correspond to different classical voting rules, allowing for explicit control over preference aggregation axioms.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了差分投票（Differential Voting），这是一个用于设计RLHF中可微损失函数的框架，该损失函数对应于不同的经典投票规则，从而可以显式控制偏好聚合的公理。
summary_zh: 从人类反馈中进行强化学习（RLHF）隐式地将异构的人类偏好聚合为单一效用函数，尽管实际上参与者的潜在效用是多样的。 因此，RLHF可以被视为一种投票形式，其中聚合机制由损失函数定义。 尽管Arrow不可能定理表明不同的机制满足不同组的期望公理，但大多数现有方法依赖于单一的聚合原则，通常是Bradley-Terry-Luce（BTL）模型，该模型对应于Borda计数投票。 这限制了学习到的奖励的公理性质，并模糊了优化中嵌入的规范性假设。 在这项工作中，我们引入了微分投票，这是一个统一的框架，它构建了实例相关的、可微分的损失函数，其群体层面的最优解可以被证明对应于不同的经典投票规则。 我们开发了基于多数原则的聚合（BTL）、Copeland和Kemeny规则的可微分代理函数，并正式分析了它们的校准性质、梯度场以及平滑参数消失时的极限行为。 对于每个损失函数，我们都建立了与相应社会选择规则的一致性，并描述了它满足或违反的公理。 我们的分析表明，损失几何中的设计选择，例如边际敏感度和边界集中度，如何直接转化为规范性聚合行为。 微分投票使偏好聚合成为RLHF中显式且可控的设计选择，从而可以在公理保证和优化稳定性之间实现有原则的权衡。 用于重现我们实验的代码已开源。

title: JaxARC: A High-Performance JAX-based Environment for Abstraction and Reasoning Research
summary: The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) tests AI systems' ability to perform human-like inductive reasoning from a few demonstration pairs. Existing Gymnasium-based RL environments severely limit experimental scale due to computational bottlenecks. We present JaxARC, an open-source, high-performance RL environment for ARC implemented in JAX. Its functional, stateless architecture enables massive parallelism, achieving 38-5,439x speedup over Gymnasium at matched batch sizes, with peak throughput of 790M steps/second. JaxARC supports multiple ARC datasets, flexible action spaces, composable wrappers, and configuration-driven reproducibility, enabling large-scale RL research previously computationally infeasible. JaxARC is available at https://github.com/aadimator/JaxARC.
tldr: JaxARC is a high-performance JAX-based RL environment for the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC), achieving significant speedups over Gymnasium and enabling large-scale RL research.
tldr_zh: JaxARC是一个基于JAX的高性能RL环境，用于抽象和推理语料库(ARC)，相比Gymnasium实现了显著的加速，并支持大规模的RL研究。
summary_zh: 抽象与推理语料库（ARC）测试人工智能系统从少量演示对中进行类人归纳推理的能力。 现有的基于Gymnasium的强化学习环境由于计算瓶颈严重限制了实验规模。 我们提出JaxARC，一个用JAX实现的、用于ARC的开源、高性能强化学习环境。 其函数式、无状态架构实现了大规模并行，在相同批次大小下，相比Gymnasium实现了38-5,439倍的速度提升，峰值吞吐量为7.9亿步/秒。 JaxARC支持多个ARC数据集、灵活的动作空间、可组合的封装器和配置驱动的可复现性，从而支持先前在计算上不可行的大规模强化学习研究。 JaxARC可在https://github.com/aadimator/JaxARC获取。

title: Towards Generalisable Imitation Learning Through Conditioned Transition Estimation and Online Behaviour Alignment
summary: State-of-the-art imitation learning from observation methods (ILfO) have recently made significant progress, but they still have some limitations: they need action-based supervised optimisation, assume that states have a single optimal action, and tend to apply teacher actions without full consideration of the actual environment state. While the truth may be out there in observed trajectories, existing methods struggle to extract it without supervision. In this work, we propose Unsupervised Imitation Learning from Observation (UfO) that addresses all of these limitations. UfO learns a policy through a two-stage process, in which the agent first obtains an approximation of the teacher's true actions in the observed state transitions, and then refines the learned policy further by adjusting agent trajectories to closely align them with the teacher's. Experiments we conducted in five widely used environments show that UfO not only outperforms the teacher and all other ILfO methods but also displays the smallest standard deviation. This reduction in standard deviation indicates better generalisation in unseen scenarios.
tldr: This paper introduces Unsupervised Imitation Learning from Observation (UfO), a two-stage method that outperforms existing ILfO approaches and demonstrates better generalization by first approximating teacher actions and then aligning agent trajectories.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种无监督模仿学习方法（UfO），它通过两阶段过程优于现有的ILfO方法，并展示了更好的泛化能力：首先近似教师的动作，然后调整agent轨迹以与教师轨迹对齐。
summary_zh: 当前最先进的基于观察的模仿学习(ILfO)方法近年来取得了显著进展，但仍然存在一些局限性：它们需要基于动作的监督优化，假设状态具有唯一的最佳动作，并且倾向于在没有充分考虑实际环境状态的情况下应用教师动作。虽然真理可能隐藏在观察到的轨迹中，但现有方法难以在没有监督的情况下提取它。在这项工作中，我们提出了无监督的基于观察的模仿学习(UfO)，它解决了所有这些局限性。UfO通过一个两阶段过程学习策略，其中智能体首先在观察到的状态转移中获得教师真实动作的近似值，然后通过调整智能体轨迹，使其与教师的轨迹紧密对齐，从而进一步改进学习到的策略。我们在五个广泛使用的环境中进行的实验表明，UfO不仅超越了教师和所有其他ILfO方法，而且表现出最小的标准差。标准差的减小表明在未见场景中具有更好的泛化能力。

title: MV-SAM: Multi-view Promptable Segmentation using Pointmap Guidance
summary: Promptable segmentation has emerged as a powerful paradigm in computer vision, enabling users to guide models in parsing complex scenes with prompts such as clicks, boxes, or textual cues. Recent advances, exemplified by the Segment Anything Model (SAM), have extended this paradigm to videos and multi-view images. However, the lack of 3D awareness often leads to inconsistent results, necessitating costly per-scene optimization to enforce 3D consistency. In this work, we introduce MV-SAM, a framework for multi-view segmentation that achieves 3D consistency using pointmaps -- 3D points reconstructed from unposed images by recent visual geometry models. Leveraging the pixel-point one-to-one correspondence of pointmaps, MV-SAM lifts images and prompts into 3D space, eliminating the need for explicit 3D networks or annotated 3D data. Specifically, MV-SAM extends SAM by lifting image embeddings from its pretrained encoder into 3D point embeddings, which are decoded by a transformer using cross-attention with 3D prompt embeddings. This design aligns 2D interactions with 3D geometry, enabling the model to implicitly learn consistent masks across views through 3D positional embeddings. Trained on the SA-1B dataset, our method generalizes well across domains, outperforming SAM2-Video and achieving comparable performance with per-scene optimization baselines on NVOS, SPIn-NeRF, ScanNet++, uCo3D, and DL3DV benchmarks. Code will be released.
tldr: MV-SAM introduces a method for multi-view segmentation using pointmaps to achieve 3D consistency, extending SAM's capabilities without requiring explicit 3D networks or data, and demonstrating good generalization across datasets.
tldr_zh: MV-SAM提出了一种使用点云地图进行多视角分割的方法，以实现3D一致性。它扩展了SAM的功能，无需显式的3D网络或数据，并在多个数据集上展示了良好的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 提示式分割已成为计算机视觉中一种强大的范式，它使用户能够通过点击、框或文本提示等方式引导模型解析复杂场景。以 Segment Anything Model (SAM) 为例，最近的进展已将这种范式扩展到视频和多视角图像。然而，缺乏 3D 感知通常会导致不一致的结果，需要昂贵的逐场景优化来强制执行 3D 一致性。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 MV-SAM，一个用于多视角分割的框架，它利用点云图来实现 3D 一致性。点云图是使用最新的视觉几何模型从无位姿图像重建的 3D 点。通过利用点云图像素点与 3D 点的一对一对应关系，MV-SAM 将图像和提示提升到 3D 空间，从而无需显式的 3D 网络或带标注的 3D 数据。具体而言，MV-SAM 通过将其预训练编码器的图像嵌入提升到 3D 点嵌入来扩展 SAM，这些点嵌入通过 Transformer 使用与 3D 提示嵌入的交叉注意力进行解码。这种设计使 2D 交互与 3D 几何对齐，使模型能够通过 3D 位置嵌入隐式地学习跨视角的一致掩码。在 SA-1B 数据集上训练后，我们的方法在各个领域都表现出良好的泛化能力，性能优于 SAM2-Video，并在 NVOS、SPIn-NeRF、ScanNet++、uCo3D 和 DL3DV 基准测试中实现了与逐场景优化基线相当的性能。代码将会发布。

title: Entropic Risk-Aware Monte Carlo Tree Search
summary: We propose a provably correct Monte Carlo tree search (MCTS) algorithm for solving \textit{risk-aware} Markov decision processes (MDPs) with \textit{entropic risk measure} (ERM) objectives. We provide a \textit{non-asymptotic} analysis of our proposed algorithm, showing that the algorithm: (i) is \textit{correct} in the sense that the empirical ERM obtained at the root node converges to the optimal ERM; and (ii) enjoys \textit{polynomial regret concentration}. Our algorithm successfully exploits the dynamic programming formulations for solving risk-aware MDPs with ERM objectives introduced by previous works in the context of an upper confidence bound-based tree search algorithm. Finally, we provide a set of illustrative experiments comparing our risk-aware MCTS method against relevant baselines.
tldr: This paper introduces a provably correct Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) algorithm for solving risk-aware Markov Decision Processes with entropic risk measures, providing non-asymptotic analysis and demonstrating polynomial regret concentration.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种可证明正确的蒙特卡洛树搜索 (MCTS) 算法，用于求解具有熵风险度量的风险感知马尔可夫决策过程，提供了非渐进分析并证明了多项式遗憾集中。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种可证明正确的蒙特卡洛树搜索 (MCTS) 算法，用于求解以熵风险度量 (ERM) 为目标的\textit{风险感知}马尔可夫决策过程 (MDP)。我们对所提出的算法进行了\textit{非渐近}分析，表明该算法：(i) 是\textit{正确的}，即根节点处的经验 ERM 收敛到最优 ERM；并且 (ii) 具有\textit{多项式遗憾集中}。我们的算法成功地利用了先前的研究工作在基于上限置信区间的树搜索算法中引入的求解具有 ERM 目标的风险感知 MDP 的动态规划公式。最后，我们提供了一组示例性实验，将我们的风险感知 MCTS 方法与相关的基线进行比较。

title: RPNT: Robust Pre-trained Neural Transformer -- A Pathway for Generalized Motor Decoding
summary: Brain decoding aims to interpret and translate neural activity into behaviors. As such, it is imperative that decoding models are able to generalize across variations, such as recordings from different brain sites, distinct sessions, different types of behavior, and a variety of subjects. Current models can only partially address these challenges and warrant the development of pretrained neural transformer models capable to adapt and generalize. In this work, we propose RPNT - Robust Pretrained Neural Transformer, designed to achieve robust generalization through pretraining, which in turn enables effective finetuning given a downstream task. In particular, RPNT unique components include 1) Multidimensional rotary positional embedding (MRoPE) to aggregate experimental metadata such as site coordinates, session name and behavior types; 2) Context-based attention mechanism via convolution kernels operating on global attention to learn local temporal structures for handling non-stationarity of neural population activity; 3) Robust self-supervised learning (SSL) objective with uniform causal masking strategies and contrastive representations. We pretrained two separate versions of RPNT on distinct datasets a) Multi-session, multi-task, and multi-subject microelectrode benchmark; b) Multi-site recordings using high-density Neuropixel 1.0 probes. The datasets include recordings from the dorsal premotor cortex (PMd) and from the primary motor cortex (M1) regions of nonhuman primates (NHPs) as they performed reaching tasks. After pretraining, we evaluated the generalization of RPNT in cross-session, cross-type, cross-subject, and cross-site downstream behavior decoding tasks. Our results show that RPNT consistently achieves and surpasses the decoding performance of existing decoding models in all tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces RPNT, a robust pre-trained neural transformer for brain decoding that generalizes across different recording sites, sessions, behavior types, and subjects, outperforming existing decoding models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了RPNT，一种鲁棒的预训练神经Transformer，用于脑部解码，可以推广到不同的记录地点、会话、行为类型和受试者，优于现有的解码模型。
summary_zh: 脑解码旨在解释和翻译神经活动为行为。因此，解码模型必须能够泛化跨各种变化，例如来自不同脑区位点的记录、不同的会话、不同类型的行为以及不同的受试者。目前的模型只能部分地解决这些挑战，因而有必要开发能够适应和泛化的预训练神经Transformer模型。在本工作中，我们提出了RPNT - 鲁棒预训练神经Transformer，旨在通过预训练实现鲁棒的泛化，从而能够针对下游任务进行有效的微调。特别地，RPNT 独特的组件包括：1) 多维旋转位置嵌入 (MRoPE)，用于聚合实验元数据，例如位点坐标、会话名称和行为类型；2) 基于上下文的注意力机制，通过卷积核作用于全局注意力以学习局部时间结构，从而处理神经群体活动的非平稳性；3) 采用均匀因果掩蔽策略和对比表示的鲁棒自监督学习 (SSL) 目标。 我们在两个不同的数据集上预训练了RPNT的两个独立版本：a) 多会话、多任务和多受试者微电极基准；b) 使用高密度Neuropixel 1.0探针的多位点记录。这些数据集包括来自非人灵长类 (NHP) 背侧前运动皮层 (PMd) 和初级运动皮层 (M1) 区域在执行抓取任务时的记录。在预训练之后，我们评估了RPNT在跨会话、跨类型、跨受试者和跨位点的下游行为解码任务中的泛化能力。我们的结果表明，RPNT 在所有任务中均始终达到并超过现有解码模型的解码性能。

title: Deep Intrinsic Surprise-Regularized Control (DISRC): A Biologically Inspired Mechanism for Efficient Deep Q-Learning in Sparse Environments
summary: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has driven major advances in autonomous control. Still, standard Deep Q-Network (DQN) agents tend to rely on fixed learning rates and uniform update scaling, even as updates are modulated by temporal-difference (TD) error. This rigidity destabilizes convergence, especially in sparse-reward settings where feedback is infrequent. We introduce Deep Intrinsic Surprise-Regularized Control (DISRC), a biologically inspired augmentation to DQN that dynamically scales Q-updates based on latent-space surprise. DISRC encodes states via a LayerNorm-based encoder and computes a deviation-based surprise score relative to a moving latent setpoint. Each update is then scaled in proportion to both TD error and surprise intensity, promoting plasticity during early exploration and stability as familiarity increases. We evaluate DISRC on two sparse-reward MiniGrid environments, which included MiniGrid-DoorKey-8x8 and MiniGrid-LavaCrossingS9N1, under identical settings as a vanilla DQN baseline. In DoorKey, DISRC reached the first successful episode (reward > 0.8) 33% faster than the vanilla DQN baseline (79 vs. 118 episodes), with lower reward standard deviation (0.25 vs. 0.34) and higher reward area under the curve (AUC: 596.42 vs. 534.90). These metrics reflect faster, more consistent learning - critical for sparse, delayed reward settings. In LavaCrossing, DISRC achieved a higher final reward (0.95 vs. 0.93) and the highest AUC of all agents (957.04), though it converged more gradually. These preliminary results establish DISRC as a novel mechanism for regulating learning intensity in off-policy agents, improving both efficiency and stability in sparse-reward domains. By treating surprise as an intrinsic learning signal, DISRC enables agents to modulate updates based on expectation violations, enhancing decision quality when conventional value-based methods fall short.
tldr: The paper introduces Deep Intrinsic Surprise-Regularized Control (DISRC), a biologically inspired augmentation to DQN that dynamically scales Q-updates based on latent-space surprise, improving efficiency and stability in sparse-reward environments. DISRC outperforms a vanilla DQN baseline in two sparse-reward MiniGrid environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了深度内在惊奇正则化控制 (DISRC)，这是一种受生物学启发的 DQN 增强方法，它基于潜在空间的惊奇程度动态地调整 Q 值更新，从而提高稀疏奖励环境中的效率和稳定性。DISRC 在两个稀疏奖励的 MiniGrid 环境中优于普通的 DQN 基线。
summary_zh: 深度强化学习（DRL）推动了自主控制领域的重大进展。然而，标准的深度Q网络（DQN）Agent倾向于依赖固定的学习率和均匀的更新尺度，即使更新已被时序差分（TD）误差所调节。这种刚性会破坏收敛性，尤其是在反馈不频繁的稀疏奖励环境中。我们引入了深度内在惊奇正则化控制（DISRC），这是一种受生物学启发的DQN增强方法，它基于潜在空间惊奇动态地缩放Q-更新。DISRC通过基于LayerNorm的编码器来编码状态，并相对于移动的潜在设定点计算基于偏差的惊奇评分。然后，每个更新都按比例缩放，既与TD误差又与惊奇强度成比例，从而在早期探索期间促进可塑性，并在熟悉程度增加时促进稳定性。我们使用与原始DQN基线相同的设置，在两个稀疏奖励的MiniGrid环境中评估了DISRC，包括MiniGrid-DoorKey-8x8和MiniGrid-LavaCrossingS9N1。在DoorKey中，DISRC达到第一个成功的episode（奖励 > 0.8）的速度比原始DQN基线快33%（79个episode vs. 118个episode），奖励标准差更低（0.25 vs. 0.34），曲线下面积（AUC）更高（596.42 vs. 534.90）。这些指标反映了更快、更一致的学习——这对于稀疏、延迟的奖励设置至关重要。在LavaCrossing中，DISRC实现了更高的最终奖励（0.95 vs. 0.93）和所有Agent中最高的AUC（957.04），尽管收敛速度较慢。这些初步结果表明，DISRC是一种新颖的机制，可以调节离线策略Agent中的学习强度，从而提高稀疏奖励领域的效率和稳定性。通过将惊奇视为一种内在的学习信号，DISRC使Agent能够根据期望违背来调节更新，从而在传统的基于价值的方法不足时提高决策质量。

title: Faramesh: A Protocol-Agnostic Execution Control Plane for Autonomous Agent Systems
summary: Autonomous agent systems increasingly trigger real-world side effects: deploying infrastructure, modifying databases, moving money, and executing workflows. Yet most agent stacks provide no mandatory execution checkpoint where organizations can deterministically permit, deny, or defer an action before it changes reality. This paper introduces Faramesh, a protocol-agnostic execution control plane that enforces execution-time authorization for agent-driven actions via a non-bypassable Action Authorization Boundary (AAB). Faramesh canonicalizes agent intent into a Canonical Action Representation (CAR), evaluates actions deterministically against policy and state, and issues a decision artifact (PERMIT/DEFER/DENY) that executors must validate prior to execution. The system is designed to be framework- and model-agnostic, supports multi-agent and multi-tenant deployments, and remains independent of transport protocols (e.g., MCP). Faramesh further provides decision-centric, append-only provenance logging keyed by canonical action hashes, enabling auditability, verification, and deterministic replay without re-running agent reasoning. We show how these primitives yield enforceable, predictable governance for autonomous execution while avoiding hidden coupling to orchestration layers or observability-only approaches.
tldr: The paper introduces Faramesh, an execution control plane for autonomous agent systems that enforces authorization policies before actions are executed, offering enhanced governance and auditability.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Faramesh的执行控制平面，用于自主Agent系统，它在操作执行前强制执行授权策略，从而提高治理和可审计性。
summary_zh: 自主代理系统日益频繁地触发真实世界的副作用：部署基础设施、修改数据库、转移资金以及执行工作流程。然而，大多数代理栈并没有提供强制执行检查点，以供组织在改变现实之前确定性地允许、拒绝或延迟某个动作。本文介绍了 Faramesh，一种与协议无关的执行控制平面，它通过不可绕过的动作授权边界（AAB）来强制执行代理驱动动作的执行时授权。Faramesh 将代理意图规范化为规范动作表示（CAR），根据策略和状态确定性地评估动作，并发布决策工件（允许/延迟/拒绝），执行器在执行之前必须验证该工件。该系统被设计为框架和模型无关，支持多代理和多租户部署，并且独立于传输协议（如MCP）。Faramesh 进一步提供以决策为中心的、仅追加来源日志记录，该日志记录通过规范动作散列进行键控，从而实现可审计性、可验证性和确定性重放，而无需重新运行代理推理。我们展示了这些原语如何在避免与编排层隐藏耦合或仅可观察性方法的同时，为自主执行产生可强制执行的、可预测的治理。

title: DIML: Differentiable Inverse Mechanism Learning from Behaviors of Multi-Agent Learning Trajectories
summary: We study inverse mechanism learning: recovering an unknown incentive-generating mechanism from observed strategic interaction traces of self-interested learning agents. Unlike inverse game theory and multi-agent inverse reinforcement learning, which typically infer utility/reward parameters inside a structured mechanism, our target includes unstructured mechanism -- a (possibly neural) mapping from joint actions to per-agent payoffs. Unlike differentiable mechanism design, which optimizes mechanisms forward, we infer mechanisms from behavior in an observational setting. We propose DIML, a likelihood-based framework that differentiates through a model of multi-agent learning dynamics and uses the candidate mechanism to generate counterfactual payoffs needed to predict observed actions. We establish identifiability of payoff differences under a conditional logit response model and prove statistical consistency of maximum likelihood estimation under standard regularity conditions. We evaluate DIML with simulated interactions of learning agents across unstructured neural mechanisms, congestion tolling, public goods subsidies, and large-scale anonymous games. DIML reliably recovers identifiable incentive differences and supports counterfactual prediction, where its performance rivals tabular enumeration oracle in small environments and its convergence scales to large, hundred-participant environments. Code to reproduce our experiments is open-sourced.
tldr: This paper introduces DIML, a method for inferring unknown incentive mechanisms (possibly neural networks) from observed multi-agent learning behavior, showing strong empirical results in recovering payoff differences and predicting counterfactuals.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了DIML，一种从观察到的多智能体学习行为中推断未知激励机制（可能是神经网络）的方法，并在恢复收益差异和预测反事实方面表现出强大的实证结果。
summary_zh: 我们研究逆机制学习：从自利学习智能体观察到的策略互动轨迹中恢复一个未知的激励生成机制。不同于逆博弈论和多智能体逆强化学习，它们通常推断结构化机制内的效用函数/奖励参数，我们的目标包含非结构化机制——一种从联合行动到每个智能体收益的（可能是神经的）映射。不同于可微机制设计，它正向优化机制，我们从观测环境中的行为推断机制。我们提出DIML，一个基于似然的框架，它通过多智能体学习动态模型进行微分，并使用候选机制来生成预测观察到的行动所需的反事实收益。我们建立了条件logit响应模型下收益差异的可辨识性，并在标准正则性条件下证明了最大似然估计的统计一致性。我们使用学习智能体在不同非结构化神经机制、拥堵收费、公共产品补贴和大规模匿名博弈中的模拟互动来评估DIML。DIML可靠地恢复可辨识的激励差异并支持反事实预测，其性能在小型环境中与表格枚举 oracle 相媲美，并且其收敛性可扩展到大型、百人参与者的环境中。复现我们实验的代码已开源。

title: Delay-Compensated Stiffness Estimation for Robot-Mediated Dyadic Interaction
summary: Robot-mediated human-human (dyadic) interactions enable therapists to provide physical therapy remotely, yet an accurate perception of patient stiffness remains challenging due to network-induced haptic delays. Conventional stiffness estimation methods, which neglect delay, suffer from temporal misalignment between force and position signals, leading to significant estimation errors as delays increase. To address this, we propose a robust, delay-compensated stiffness estimation framework by deriving an algebraic estimator based on quasi-static equilibrium that explicitly accounts for temporally aligning the expert's input with the novice's response. A Normalised Weighted Least Squares (NWLS) implementation is then introduced to robustly filter dynamic bias resulting from the algebraic derivation. Experiments using commercial rehabilitation robots (H-MAN) as the platform demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms the standard estimator, maintaining consistent tracking accuracy under multiple introduced delays. These findings offer a promising solution for achieving high-fidelity haptic perception in remote dyadic interaction, potentially facilitating reliable stiffness assessment in therapeutic settings across networks.
tldr: This paper proposes a delay-compensated stiffness estimation framework for robot-mediated dyadic interaction in remote therapy settings, addressing the challenges posed by network-induced haptic delays. The method shows improved tracking accuracy compared to standard estimators.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于远程治疗环境中机器人介导的二元交互的延迟补偿刚度估计框架，解决了由网络引起的触觉延迟带来的挑战。该方法显示出比标准估计器更高的跟踪精度。
summary_zh: 机器人辅助的人人（二元）交互使治疗师能够进行远程物理治疗，但由于网络引起的触觉延迟，对患者刚度的准确感知仍然具有挑战性。忽略延迟的传统刚度估算方法会遭受力和位置信号之间的时间错位，导致随着延迟增加而产生显著的估算误差。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种鲁棒的、延迟补偿的刚度估算框架，通过基于准静态平衡推导出一种代数估算器，该估算器显式考虑了专家输入与新手响应之间的时间对齐。然后，引入一种归一化加权最小二乘法（NWLS）实现，以可靠地过滤由代数推导产生的动态偏差。使用商用康复机器人（H-MAN）作为平台的实验表明，所提出的方法显著优于标准估算器，在多种引入的延迟下保持一致的跟踪精度。这些发现为在远程二元交互中实现高保真触觉感知提供了一种有前景的解决方案，可能有助于在跨网络的治疗环境中进行可靠的刚度评估。

title: FedCCA: Client-Centric Adaptation against Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning on IoT Devices
summary: With the rapid development of the Internet of Things (IoT), AI model training on private data such as human sensing data is highly desired. Federated learning (FL) has emerged as a privacy-preserving distributed training framework for this purpuse. However, the data heterogeneity issue among IoT devices can significantly degrade the model performance and convergence speed in FL. Existing approaches limit in fixed client selection and aggregation on cloud server, making the privacy-preserving extraction of client-specific information during local training challenging. To this end, we propose Client-Centric Adaptation federated learning (FedCCA), an algorithm that optimally utilizes client-specific knowledge to learn a unique model for each client through selective adaptation, aiming to alleviate the influence of data heterogeneity. Specifically, FedCCA employs dynamic client selection and adaptive aggregation based on the additional client-specific encoder. To enhance multi-source knowledge transfer, we adopt an attention-based global aggregation strategy. We conducted extensive experiments on diverse datasets to assess the efficacy of FedCCA. The experimental results demonstrate that our approach exhibits a substantial performance advantage over competing baselines in addressing this specific problem.
tldr: The paper introduces FedCCA, a federated learning algorithm that uses client-specific encoders and adaptive aggregation to mitigate data heterogeneity in IoT devices, demonstrating superior performance over baselines.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 FedCCA，一种联邦学习算法，它使用客户端特定编码器和自适应聚合来缓解物联网设备中的数据异构性，并表现出优于基线的性能。
summary_zh: 随着物联网（IoT）的快速发展，对人类感知数据等私有数据进行AI模型训练的需求日益增长。联邦学习（FL）作为一种保护隐私的分布式训练框架应运而生，以满足这一需求。然而，IoT设备间的数据异构性会显著降低FL中的模型性能和收敛速度。现有的方法局限于固定的客户端选择和云服务器上的聚合，使得在本地训练期间进行保护隐私的客户端特定信息提取变得具有挑战性。为此，我们提出了一种以客户端为中心的自适应联邦学习（FedCCA）算法，该算法通过选择性自适应，最优地利用客户端特定知识来为每个客户端学习一个独特的模型，从而缓解数据异构性的影响。具体来说，FedCCA采用基于附加的客户端特定编码器的动态客户端选择和自适应聚合。为了增强多源知识转移，我们采用了一种基于注意力机制的全局聚合策略。我们在不同的数据集上进行了广泛的实验，以评估FedCCA的有效性。实验结果表明，在解决这一特定问题上，我们的方法相对于其他基线方法表现出显著的性能优势。

title: BrainDistill: Implantable Motor Decoding with Task-Specific Knowledge Distillation
summary: Transformer-based neural decoders with large parameter counts, pre-trained on large-scale datasets, have recently outperformed classical machine learning models and small neural networks on brain-computer interface (BCI) tasks. However, their large parameter counts and high computational demands hinder deployment in power-constrained implantable systems. To address this challenge, we introduce BrainDistill, a novel implantable motor decoding pipeline that integrates an implantable neural decoder (IND) with a task-specific knowledge distillation (TSKD) framework. Unlike standard feature distillation methods that attempt to preserve teacher representations in full, TSKD explicitly prioritizes features critical for decoding through supervised projection. Across multiple neural datasets, IND consistently outperforms prior neural decoders on motor decoding tasks, while its TSKD-distilled variant further surpasses alternative distillation methods in few-shot calibration settings. Finally, we present a quantization-aware training scheme that enables integer-only inference with activation clipping ranges learned during training. The quantized IND enables deployment under the strict power constraints of implantable BCIs with minimal performance loss.
tldr: The paper introduces BrainDistill, an implantable motor decoding pipeline that uses task-specific knowledge distillation and quantization-aware training to enable efficient deployment of transformer-based neural decoders in power-constrained implantable BCIs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 BrainDistill，一种植入式运动解码管道，它使用任务特定的知识蒸馏和量化感知训练，从而能够在功耗受限的植入式脑机接口中高效部署基于 Transformer 的神经解码器。
summary_zh: 基于Transformer的神经解码器，拥有大量参数并在大规模数据集上进行了预训练，最近在脑机接口（BCI）任务中超越了经典的机器学习模型和小型神经网络。然而，其庞大的参数量和高计算需求阻碍了在功耗受限的可植入系统中的部署。为了应对这一挑战，我们介绍BrainDistill，一种新型的可植入运动解码流程，它集成了可植入神经解码器（IND）和一个任务特定知识蒸馏（TSKD）框架。与试图完整保留教师模型表征的标准特征蒸馏方法不同，TSKD通过监督投影显式地优先考虑对解码至关重要的特征。在多个神经数据集上，IND在运动解码任务中始终优于先前的神经解码器，而其TSKD蒸馏变体在少样本校准设置中进一步超越了其他蒸馏方法。最后，我们提出了一种量化感知训练方案，该方案能够在训练期间学习激活剪裁范围，从而实现纯整数推理。量化的IND能够在可植入BCI严格的功耗约束下进行部署，且性能损失极小。

title: EEG Foundation Models: Progresses, Benchmarking, and Open Problems
summary: Electroencephalography (EEG) foundation models have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), aiming to learn transferable neural representations from large-scale heterogeneous recordings. Despite rapid progresses, there lacks fair and comprehensive comparisons of existing EEG foundation models, due to inconsistent pre-training objectives, preprocessing choices, and downstream evaluation protocols. This paper fills this gap. We first review 50 representative models and organize their design choices into a unified taxonomic framework including data standardization, model architectures, and self-supervised pre-training strategies. We then evaluate 12 open-source foundation models and competitive specialist baselines across 13 EEG datasets spanning nine BCI paradigms. Emphasizing real-world deployments, we consider both cross-subject generalization under a leave-one-subject-out protocol and rapid calibration under a within-subject few-shot setting. We further compare full-parameter fine-tuning with linear probing to assess the transferability of pre-trained representations, and examine the relationship between model scale and downstream performance. Our results indicate that: 1) linear probing is frequently insufficient; 2) specialist models trained from scratch remain competitive across many tasks; and, 3) larger foundation models do not necessarily yield better generalization performance under current data regimes and training practices.
tldr: This paper reviews and benchmarks EEG foundation models, finding that linear probing is often insufficient, specialist models are competitive, and larger models don't always generalize better.
tldr_zh: 该论文综述并评估了脑电图（EEG）基础模型，发现线性探针通常不足，专家模型具有竞争力，且更大的模型并不总是能带来更好的泛化性能。
summary_zh: 脑电图(EEG)基础模型最近作为一种有前景的脑机接口(BCI)范式涌现，旨在从大规模异构记录中学习可迁移的神经表征。尽管取得了快速进展，但由于预训练目标、预处理选择和下游评估协议的不一致，目前缺乏对现有脑电图基础模型的公平和全面比较。本文填补了这一空白。我们首先回顾了50个具有代表性的模型，并将它们的设计选择组织成一个统一的分类框架，包括数据标准化、模型架构和自监督预训练策略。然后，我们在涵盖九种脑机接口范式的13个脑电图数据集上，评估了12个开源基础模型和有竞争力的专业基线模型。为了强调实际部署，我们考虑了在留一被试外的协议下的跨被试泛化，以及在被试内的少样本设置下的快速校准。我们进一步比较了全参数微调与线性探测，以评估预训练表征的可迁移性，并检查了模型规模与下游性能之间的关系。我们的结果表明：1) 线性探测通常是不够的；2) 从头开始训练的专业模型在许多任务中仍然具有竞争力；3) 在当前的数据机制和训练实践下，更大的基础模型不一定能产生更好的泛化性能。

title: Agreement-Driven Multi-View 3D Reconstruction for Live Cattle Weight Estimation
summary: Accurate cattle live weight estimation is vital for livestock management, welfare, and productivity. Traditional methods, such as manual weighing using a walk-over weighing system or proximate measurements using body condition scoring, involve manual handling of stock and can impact productivity from both a stock and economic perspective. To address these issues, this study investigated a cost-effective, non-contact method for live weight calculation in cattle using 3D reconstruction. The proposed pipeline utilized multi-view RGB images with SAM 3D-based agreement-guided fusion, followed by ensemble regression. Our approach generates a single 3D point cloud per animal and compares classical ensemble models with deep learning models under low-data conditions. Results show that SAM 3D with multi-view agreement fusion outperforms other 3D generation methods, while classical ensemble models provide the most consistent performance for practical farm scenarios (R$^2$ = 0.69 $\pm$ 0.10, MAPE = 2.22 $\pm$ 0.56 \%), making this practical for on-farm implementation. These findings demonstrate that improving reconstruction quality is more critical than increasing model complexity for scalable deployment on farms where producing a large volume of 3D data is challenging.
tldr: This paper proposes a cost-effective, non-contact method utilizing multi-view 3D reconstruction with SAM-guided fusion and ensemble regression for cattle live weight estimation, showing classical ensemble models outperform deep learning under low-data conditions for on-farm implementation.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种经济高效的非接触式方法，利用多视图3D重建与SAM引导的融合和集成回归来估计牛的活重。结果表明，在低数据条件下，经典的集成模型优于深度学习，适用于农场实施。
summary_zh: 准确的牛活体重估计对于牲畜管理、福利和生产力至关重要。传统方法，如使用走道式称重系统进行人工称重或使用体况评分进行近似测量，涉及人工处理牲畜，并且可能从牲畜和经济角度影响生产力。为了解决这些问题，本研究探讨了一种经济高效、非接触式的牛活体重计算方法，该方法利用3D重建技术。所提出的流程利用多视角RGB图像，结合基于SAM 3D的具有一致性引导的融合，然后进行集成回归。我们的方法为每只动物生成一个单独的3D点云，并在低数据条件下比较经典集成模型与深度学习模型。结果表明，具有多视角一致性融合的SAM 3D优于其他3D生成方法，而经典集成模型为实际农场场景提供了最稳定的性能（R$^2$ = 0.69 $\pm$ 0.10，MAPE = 2.22 $\pm$ 0.56 %），使其适用于农场实施。这些发现表明，对于在农场上可扩展部署而言，提高重建质量比增加模型复杂性更为关键，因为在农场上生成大量的3D数据具有挑战性。

title: Learning Sewing Patterns via Latent Flow Matching of Implicit Fields
summary: Sewing patterns define the structural foundation of garments and are essential for applications such as fashion design, fabrication, and physical simulation. Despite progress in automated pattern generation, accurately modeling sewing patterns remains difficult due to the broad variability in panel geometry and seam arrangements. In this work, we introduce a sewing pattern modeling method based on an implicit representation. We represent each panel using a signed distance field that defines its boundary and an unsigned distance field that identifies seam endpoints, and encode these fields into a continuous latent space that enables differentiable meshing. A latent flow matching model learns distributions over panel combinations in this representation, and a stitching prediction module recovers seam relations from extracted edge segments. This formulation allows accurate modeling and generation of sewing patterns with complex structures. We further show that it can be used to estimate sewing patterns from images with improved accuracy relative to existing approaches, and supports applications such as pattern completion and refitting, providing a practical tool for digital fashion design.
tldr: The paper introduces a novel sewing pattern modeling method using implicit representations and latent flow matching to accurately model and generate sewing patterns, even from images, with applications in digital fashion design.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新的缝纫模式建模方法，该方法利用隐式表示和潜在流匹配来精确地建模和生成缝纫模式，甚至可以从图像中生成，并应用在数字时尚设计中。
summary_zh: 缝纫纸样定义了服装的结构基础，对于时尚设计、制作和物理仿真等应用至关重要。尽管自动纸样生成方面取得了进展，但由于裁片几何形状和缝合方式的广泛可变性，准确建模缝纫纸样仍然很困难。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于隐式表示的缝纫纸样建模方法。我们使用一个有符号距离场表示每个裁片，该距离场定义了它的边界，并使用一个无符号距离场来识别缝合端点，并将这些场编码成一个连续的潜在空间，从而实现可微网格划分。一个潜在流匹配模型学习了这种表示中的裁片组合的分布，而一个缝合预测模块从提取的边缘段中恢复缝合关系。这种公式允许准确地建模和生成具有复杂结构的缝纫纸样。我们进一步表明，它可以用于从图像中估计缝纫纸样，相对于现有方法具有更高的精度，并支持纸样补全和重新拟合等应用，为数字时尚设计提供了一个实用的工具。

title: ME-WARD: A multimodal ergonomic analysis tool for musculoskeletal risk assessment from inertial and video data in working plac
summary: This study presents ME-WARD (Multimodal Ergonomic Workplace Assessment and Risk from Data), a novel system for ergonomic assessment and musculoskeletal risk evaluation that implements the Rapid Upper Limb Assessment (RULA) method. ME-WARD is designed to process joint angle data from motion capture systems, including inertial measurement unit (IMU)-based setups, and deep learning human body pose tracking models. The tool's flexibility enables ergonomic risk assessment using any system capable of reliably measuring joint angles, extending the applicability of RULA beyond proprietary setups. To validate its performance, the tool was tested in an industrial setting during the assembly of conveyor belts, which involved high-risk tasks such as inserting rods and pushing conveyor belt components. The experiments leveraged gold standard IMU systems alongside a state-of-the-art monocular 3D pose estimation system. The results confirmed that ME-WARD produces reliable RULA scores that closely align with IMU-derived metrics for flexion-dominated movements and comparable performance with the monocular system, despite limitations in tracking lateral and rotational motions. This work highlights the potential of integrating multiple motion capture technologies into a unified and accessible ergonomic assessment pipeline. By supporting diverse input sources, including low-cost video-based systems, the proposed multimodal approach offers a scalable, cost-effective solution for ergonomic assessments, paving the way for broader adoption in resource-constrained industrial environments.
tldr: The paper introduces ME-WARD, a multimodal ergonomic assessment tool that uses IMU and video data to evaluate musculoskeletal risk via the RULA method, validated in an industrial setting.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为ME-WARD的多模态人体工程学评估工具，该工具使用IMU和视频数据，通过快速上肢评估（RULA）方法来评估肌肉骨骼风险，并在工业环境中进行了验证。
summary_zh: 本研究提出了一种名为ME-WARD（数据驱动的多模态人体工效学工作场所评估与风险）的新型系统，用于人体工效学评估和肌肉骨骼风险评估，该系统实现了快速上肢评估（RULA）方法。 ME-WARD旨在处理来自运动捕捉系统的关节角度数据，包括基于惯性测量单元（IMU）的设置和深度学习人体姿势跟踪模型。该工具的灵活性使其能够使用任何能够可靠测量关节角度的系统进行人体工效学风险评估，从而将RULA的应用范围扩展到专有设置之外。 为了验证其性能，该工具在工业环境中进行了测试，用于输送带的组装，其中涉及诸如插入杆和推动输送带组件等高风险任务。 实验利用了黄金标准IMU系统以及最先进的单目3D姿态估计系统。 结果证实，ME-WARD产生了可靠的RULA评分，该评分与基于IMU的屈曲主导运动指标非常吻合，并且与单目系统具有相当的性能，尽管在跟踪横向和旋转运动方面存在局限性。 这项工作强调了将多种运动捕捉技术集成到统一且易于访问的人体工效学评估流程中的潜力。 通过支持包括低成本视频系统在内的各种输入源，所提出的多模态方法为人体工效学评估提供了一种可扩展的、经济高效的解决方案，为在资源有限的工业环境中更广泛的采用铺平了道路。
